prompt: Develop a Keystone Offline Operations Management system for organizations that handle regulated records and high-volume listings, combining candidate intake, lost-and-found item tracking, and automotive part master data in one on-premise application. Users include Administrator (system configuration and access), Intake Specialist (candidate creation and document intake), Reviewer (approval workflow), Inventory Clerk (lost-and-found and part catalog upkeep), and Auditor (read-only oversight). The React web interface provides a role-based navigation experience where users can create drafts, submit for review, approve or reject with required comments, and track status changes with clear timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time. Lost-and-found posts support publish/edit/unlist/delete, must include a category tag, a validated US-style location description, and a time window; posts older than 90 days auto-unlist but remain searchable for audit. Duplicate-post prevention flags entries with the same category plus a high-similarity title within 24 hours, requiring a reviewer override. Candidate profiles capture basic demographics, initial exam scores, application details, transfer preferences, and document checklists; uploads are limited to PDF/JPG/PNG, 20 MB per file, with a required completeness status before submission. For automotive parts, staff manage fitment dimensions (model/year/engine/transmission), OEM-to-alternative mappings, attribute templates, and bulk CSV import/export with a preview-and-validate step; users can view historical versions and compare changes before promoting a new version to active. Search and reporting provide combined filters, optional fuzzy matching, configurable exports with field selection, and KPI dashboards for conversion rate, review cycle time, and quota utilization, with sensitive fields masked by default. The backend uses Go with Echo to expose offline-first REST APIs with optional OpenAPI documentation generated locally, consumed by the React frontend over the local network or localhost, keeping a decoupled architecture while requiring no internet connectivity. PostgreSQL persists all entities with strict validation rules, version retention (immutable prior versions), and referential integrity between candidates, documents, listings, and part fitment records; bulk imports run in transactions and fail atomically on schema or constraint errors. Authentication supports local username and password only, with password length minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, and optional MFA implemented via time-based one-time codes generated by a locally stored shared secret (no SMS/email). Authorization enforces RBAC down to menu visibility and data scope plus fine-grained permissions for create/approve/export/download actions. Sensitive data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 for stored secrets and masked in UI and exports, while document integrity is protected via SHA-256 hash verification and optional watermarking on generated download copies; download rights require explicit permission and are logged. Full-chain operational audit logging records logins, CRUD actions, approvals, exports, and download events with actor, timestamp, device identifier, and before/after diffs, supporting offline compliance reviews without any third-party integrations, webhooks, payments, maps, or cloud services.
